title: Measuring spectral reflectance and transmittance of small and narrow leaves
author: Etienne Laliberté
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.q56dy9e
materials: Spectra Vista Corporation DC-R/T Integrating Sphere

steps.instruction: Install the integrating sphere onto the spectroradiometer. Follow the SVC integrating sphere manual p. 6-16.
Power the spectroradiometer on and warm up for >15 min . 00:15:00, Spectroradiometer warm-up period
Screw the tethered light trap on the reflectance port sample holder. The light trap can stay on the sample holder for the entire measurement session.

Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the reflectance port . Place the standard over the reflectance port so that the light beam shines directly on its reflective surface (= facing inside of the sphere).
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the reference radiance for the filter paper in reflectance mode ( R ref,p ).
Remove the filter paper from the transmission port sample holder.
Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard from the reflectance port.
Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the transmission port .
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the stray light radiance in reflectance mode ( R str ).
Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard from the sphere reflectance port .
Remove the light trap from the transmission port sample holder.
Position the lamp over the sphere transmission port . Make sure lamp is secured in locked position. The lamp can get very hot. Grab it by the slotted heat shield.
Install the Spectralon® plug over the primary light port . Ensure that the curved plug is placed the correct way to match the curvature of the sphere.

Collect a ' Reference Scan ' in this configuration. a This corresponds to the reference radiance in transmittance mode for the leaf array ( T ref ). The reference data will be automatically saved in all successive target scan files until a new 'Reference Scan' is made.
Gently pull lamp and transmission port sample holder away from the sphere. The lamp can get very hot. Grab it by the slotted heat shield.
Place the sample platform holding leaf array #1 over the transmission port with the abaxial (lower) surfaces of the leaves facing into the sphere.
Release the transmission sample holder and move lamp back to its locked position.
Carefully replace leaf array #1 by leaf array #2.
Carefully replace leaf array #2 by leaf array #3.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf array #3 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf array #3 ( T tar,3 ).
Remove leaf array #6 from the transmission sample port holder.

The equation (Noda et al. 2013; eqn. 9) for adaxial reflectance of leaf array i , ρ a, i is ρ a, i = [( R tar,a, i − R str ) ÷ ( R ref,a − R str )] × ρ ref × [1 ÷ (1 − G r, i )] where R tar,a, i is the target radiance of leaf array i (adaxial side) in reflectance mode, R ref,a is the reference radiance used for all leaf arrays in reflectance mode, R str is the stray light radiance in reflectance mode, ρ ref is the absolute reflectance of the calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard, and G r, i is the gap fraction in reflectance mode for leaf array i , which is calculated at 400 nm (Noda et al. 2013; eqn. 13) by G r, i = [ ( ( R tar,ap, i − R str ) ÷ ( R ref,ap − R str ) ) − ( ( R tar,a, i − R str ) ÷ ( R ref,a − R str ) ) ] × ( ρ ref ÷ ρ p ) where R tar,ap, i is the target radiance of leaf array i + filter paper in reflectance mode, R ref,ap is the reference radiance used for all leaf arrays + filter paper in reflectance mode, and ρ p is the absolute reflectance of the filter paper, which is calculated (Noda et al. 2013; eqn. 3) by ρ p = [( R tar,p − R str ) ÷ ( R ref,p − R str )] × ρ ref where R tar,p is the target radiance of the filter paper in reflectance mode, and R ref,p is the reference radiance of the filter paper in reflectance mode. Leaf arrays 1-3 use the first R ref,a and R ref,ap measurements, whereas leaf arrays 4-6 use the second measurements.

Build at least of six sample mounts from thin plastic (e.g. acetate sheets). a The sample mount is made of a thin plastic sheet with a square window that is larger than the edge of the sample port lip (e.g. 2.5 cm × 2.5 cm). Fig. 3a from Noda et al. (2013). https://doi.org/10.1111/pce.12100

Build at least six few sample platforms from thin cardboard (e.g. file folders). a The sample platform is made of cardboard and will eventually attach to the integrating sphere to create a flat surface. Fig. 3c from Noda et al. (2013). https://doi.org/10.1111/pce.12100

Fix leaves onto the sample mount with tape to make leaf array #1. a Fig. 3b from Noda et al. (2013). https://doi.org/10.1111/pce.12100 Try to position as much leaf material as possible in the center of the port where the intensity of the light beam will be highest. Leaves should be separated by about 1 mm to avoid multiple scattering among them. If leaves are shorter than the sample mount hole (i.e. <1.25 cm long), fix leaves by their petiole with tape in one (or two) rows.

Fix leaves onto the sample mount with tape to make leaf array #2. Try to position as much leaf material as possible in the center of the port where the intensity of the light beam will be highest. Leaves should be separated by about 1 mm to avoid multiple scattering among them. If leaves are shorter than the sample mount hole (i.e. <1.25 cm long), fix leaves by their petiole with tape in one (or two) rows.

Fix leaves onto the sample mount with tape to make leaf array #3. Try to position as much leaf material as possible in the center of the port where the intensity of the light beam will be highest. Leaves should be separated by about 1 mm to avoid multiple scattering among them. If leaves are shorter than the sample mount hole (i.e. <1.25 cm long), fix leaves by their petiole with tape in one (or two) rows.
Fix sample mount holding leaf array #1 onto the sample platform with tape. The sample mount holding the leaf array should be sandwiched between the two sides of the cardboard sample platform.
Fix sample mount holding leaf array #2 onto the sample platform with tape. The sample mount holding the leaf array should be sandwiched between the two sides of the cardboard sample platform.
Fix sample mount holding leaf array #3 onto the sample platform with tape. The sample mount holding the leaf array should be sandwiched between the two sides of the cardboard sample platform.
Carefully remove the sample platform holding leaf array #1 from the transmission port. The filter paper should remain in the same position.
Position leaf array #1 over the transmission port so that the adaxial (upper) surfaces of the leaves face into the sphere.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in the same configuration and save the file .
Place a filter paper directly behind the sample platform holding leaf array #1. The leaf array #1 should remain in the same position.
Place the filter paper over the reflectance port . Focus the measurements on the same area of the paper used in previous measurements.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of the filter paper in reflectance mode ( R tar,p ).
Position the sample platform containing leaf array #1 in front the filter paper over the reflectance port with the adaxial (upper) surfaces of the leaves face into the sphere.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #1 + paper in reflectance mode ( R tar,ap,1 ).
Carefully replace leaf array #1 by leaf array #2.

The equation for adaxial transmittance of leaf array i , τ a, i is (modified from eqn. 15 of Noda et al. 2013 for the SVC sphere) τ a, i = [ ( T tar, i ÷ T ref ) − G t, i ] × [ 1 ÷ (1 − G t, i ) ] where T tar, i is the target radiance of leaf array i in transmittance mode, T ref is the reference radiance used for all leaf arrays in transmittance mode, G t, i is the gap fraction in transmittance mode for leaf array i , which is calculated at 400 nm (since τ a, i = 0 at that wavelength) by G t, i = T tar, i ÷ T ref
Power the integrating sphere lamp and warm up for >5 min . 00:05:00, Integrating sphere warm-up period
Ensure that the spectroradiometer is connected to the computer via Bluetooth. The default Bluetooth password for SVC HR-1024i spectroradiometers is hr1024i .
Launch SVC Scan. a
Select auto-save mode using File > Data Options > Auto Save.

Select File > New to create a new working folder in your shared ' spectra ' Google Drive CABO folder. If you do not yet have a shared Google Drive folder for your project, contact the CABO data manager to create one ( etienne.laliberte@umontreal.ca or jeremy.goimard@umontreal.ca ). Your shared CABO Google Drive folder should be named YYYYstarted-YourLastName-Degree-University , e.g. 2018-Girard-MSc-UdeM.

Create a new folder named YYYY-MM-DD-SiteID-SpectroradiometerSerialNumber (without spaces) within that ' spectra ' folder, and keep the default file base name (= grMMDDYY ). a The base file name is the prefix for all files that will be saved within that folder. In auto-save mode, each file will be added an incremental step, e.g., gr053018_0000.sig , gr053018_0001.sig , etc.
Go to Control > Setup Instrument... to connect the spectroradiometer. a Different instruments may be configured under different virtual COM ports.
Choose the RAW DN optic Select 5 sec scan time Select Auto Integration Ensure that the date and time are correct Check Integration Scales Raw DN Data a
In Control > Overlap / Matching Settings: Select Preserve Overlapped Detector Data Select Matching Type > None a
Review the document summarising the different sphere configurations (A−I), and the scans that need to be recorded in each configuration. spectrosmallleaves-5.pdf
Screw the tethered light trap on the transmission port sample holder. The light trap can stay on the sample holder for the entire measurement session.

Check lamp alignment. Use a thin piece of paper at the exit of the reflectance sample port (empty port) the to ensure the light beam under-fills and is centered in the reflectance port. If it is not, then proceed to lamp alignment as described in the SVC integrating sphere user manual, p. 29-32.
Position the lamp over the sphere primary light entrance port . Make sure lamp is secured in locked position. The lamp can get very hot. Grab it by the slotted heat shield.
Remove the filter paper from the reflectance port. The sample platform holding leaf array #1 should remain in the same position.
Remove the filter paper from the reflectance port. The sample platform holding leaf array #2 should remain in the same position.
Place the filter paper directly behind leaf array #2 over the reflectance port .
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #2 + paper in reflectance mode ( R tar,ap,2 ).
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #2 in reflectance mode ( R tar,a,2 ).
Carefully replace leaf array #2 by leaf array #3.
Place the filter paper directly behind leaf array #3 over the reflectance port .
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #3 + paper in reflectance mode ( R tar,ap,3 ).
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #3 in reflectance mode ( R tar,a,3 ).
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #1 in reflectance mode ( R tar,a,1 ).
Remove the filter paper from the reflectance port. The sample platform holding leaf array #3 should remain in the same position.

Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the reflectance port . Place the standard over the reflectance port so that the light beam shines directly on its reflective surface (= facing inside of the sphere).
Position leaf array #1 over the transmission port so that the adaxial (upper) surfaces of the leaves face into the sphere.
Remove leaf array #3 from the reflectance port.
Remove leaf array #1 and filter paper from the transmission port .
Position the sample platform containing leaf array #1 over the reflectance port with the abaxial (lower) surface of the leaves facing into the sphere.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file .
Carefully remove leaf array #1 from the reflectance port .
Collect a ' Target Scan ' under this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf array #1 ( T tar,1 ).
Collect a ' Target Scan ' under this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf array #2 ( T tar,2 ).
Position the sample platform containing leaf array #1 over the reflectance port with the abaxial (lower) surface of the leaves facing into the sphere.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This second reference radiance is only used to assess system stability in transmittance mode.

Fix leaves onto the sample mount with tape to make leaf array #4. Try to position as much leaf material as possible in the center of the port where the intensity of the light beam will be highest. Leaves should be separated by about 1 mm to avoid multiple scattering among them. If leaves are shorter than the sample mount hole (i.e. <1.25 cm long), fix leaves by their petiole with tape in one (or two) rows.

Fix leaves onto the sample mount with tape to make leaf array #5. Try to position as much leaf material as possible in the center of the port where the intensity of the light beam will be highest. Leaves should be separated by about 1 mm to avoid multiple scattering among them. If leaves are shorter than the sample mount hole (i.e. <1.25 cm long), fix leaves by their petiole with tape in one (or two) rows.

Fix leaves onto the sample mount with tape to make leaf array #6. Try to position as much leaf material as possible in the center of the port where the intensity of the light beam will be highest. Leaves should be separated by about 1 mm to avoid multiple scattering among them. If leaves are shorter than the sample mount hole (i.e. <1.25 cm long), fix leaves by their petiole with tape in one (or two) rows.
Fix sample mount holding leaf array #4 onto the sample platform with tape. The sample mount holding the leaf array should be sandwiched between the two sides of the cardboard sample platform.
Fix sample mount holding leaf array #5 onto the sample platform with tape. The sample mount holding the leaf array should be sandwiched between the two sides of the cardboard sample platform.
Fix sample mount holding leaf array #6 onto the sample platform with tape. The sample mount holding the leaf array should be sandwiched between the two sides of the cardboard sample platform.
Place a filter paper directly behind the sample platform holding leaf array #1. The leaf array #1 should remain in the same position.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the reference radiance for the leaf array + paper in reflectance mode ( R ref,ap ) for leaf arrays 4-6.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the reference radiance in reflectance mode for the leaf array + paper ( R ref,ap ) for leaf arrays 1-3.
Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard from the transmission port .
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the reference radiance for leaf arrays 4-6 in reflectance mode ( R ref,ap ).

Collect a ' Reference Scan ' in this configuration. a This corresponds to the reference radiance in reflectance mode for the leaf arrays 1-3 ( R ref,a ). The reference data will be automatically saved in all successive target scan files until a new 'Reference Scan' is made.
Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard from the reflectance port .
Remove leaf array #1 and filter paper from the transmission port .
Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the transmission port .
Position the sample platform containing leaf array #4 in front the filter paper over the reflectance port with the adaxial (upper) surfaces of the leaves face into the sphere.
Place the filter paper over the reflectance port . Focus the measurements on the same area of the paper used in previous measurements.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #4 + paper in reflectance mode ( R tar,ap,4 ).
Remove the filter paper from the reflectance port. The sample platform holding leaf array #4 should remain in the same position.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #4 in reflectance mode ( R tar,a,4 ).
Carefully replace leaf array #4 by leaf array #5.
Place the filter paper directly behind leaf array #5 over the reflectance port . Focus the measurements on the same area of the paper used in previous measurements.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #5 + paper in reflectance mode ( R tar,ap,5 ).
Remove the filter paper from the reflectance port. The sample platform holding leaf array #5 should remain in the same position.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #5 in reflectance mode ( R tar,a,5 ).
Carefully replace leaf array #5 by leaf array #6.
Place the filter paper directly behind leaf array #6 over the reflectance port . Focus the measurements on the same area of the paper used in previous measurements.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #6 + paper in reflectance mode ( R tar,ap,6 ).
Remove the filter paper from the reflectance port. The sample platform holding leaf array #6 should remain in the same position.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This corresponds to the target radiance of leaf array #6 in reflectance mode ( R tar,a,6 ).
Remove leaf array #6 from the reflectance port.
Remove the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard from the transmission port .

Place the tethered calibrated Spectralon® reflectance standard over the reflectance port . Place the standard over the reflectance port so that the light beam shines directly on its reflective surface (= facing inside of the sphere).
Position leaf array #1 over the transmission port so that the adaxial (upper) surfaces of the leaves face into the sphere.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This third reference measurement is only used to assess system stability in reflectance mode for the leaf array reference.
Place a filter paper directly behind the sample platform holding leaf array #1. The leaf array #1 should remain in the same position.

Collect a ' Target Scan ' in this configuration and save the file . a This third reference measurement is only used to assess system stability in reflectance mode for the leaf array + paper reference.
Carefully replace leaf array #3 by leaf array #4.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf array #4 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf array #4 ( T tar,4 ).
Carefully replace leaf array #4 by leaf array #5.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf array #5 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf array #5 ( T tar,5 ).
Carefully replace leaf array #5 by leaf array #6.
Collect a ' Target Scan ' for leaf array #6 in this configuration and save the file . This corresponds to the target radiance in transmittance mode for leaf array #6 ( T tar,6 ).
